9 best things to do in marrakesh 🕌✨ 1) stay in a riad 🛏️  feel the authentic moroccan vibe, from the mosaic tiles to the whisper-quiet courtyards. riads offer privacy, fewer crowds, and a home-away-from-home feel with traditional decor, often a peaceful rooftop terrace, and staff who treat you like family. perfect for a truly moroccan experience without the hotel hustle. 2) best coffee at bacha coffee ☕️✨ (location: https://goo.gl/maps/6u3xx9j9xqv1w9qw7) silky espresso, perfectly balanced blends, and a chic, relaxed atmosphere. great latte art, friendly baristas, and a bright, stylish space to post your vibes. pro tip: try their signature crush coffee and pastry pairing. 3) explore the souks 🧿🌀 fun tip: haggle with a smile, never agree to the first price! make it a playful game, sample spices, and test your bargaining skills. lose yourself in ribbons of color, leather, pottery, and lamps. the thrill of the find is half the fun! 4) visit ben youssef madrasa 🕌 a jaw-dropping historic site with intricate tilework, carved wood, and serene courtyards. it’s a perfect spot for photos that scream old-world marrakech charm. 5) jardin majorelle 🌿💙 iconic cobalt-blue paths, lush botanical displays, and a peaceful escape from the market buzz. a must-see for color lovers and plant enthusiasts, plus it’s connected to the yves saint laurent legacy. 6) ourika valley day trip 🚙🌄 about 1 hour from central marrakech (depending on traffic). what to expect: stunning valley scenery, berber villages, waterfalls, and a cooler climate. a refreshing break from the medina with photo-worthy viewpoints. 7) agafay desert experience 🐫🏜️ highlights: camel ride under the open sky, squad  bike moments, and magical desert views. luxe touch: stay at a desert camp with plush lounges, gourmet meals, and starlit dinners. perfect for a memorable  outing. 8) dinner at la trattoria 🍝🇮🇹  warm, cozy ambiance with authentic italian flavors in marrakech. great for a relaxed evening, fresh pastas, and a lively dining vibe after a day of exploration. 9) jemaa el-fnaa - the heart of marrakech 🎭💃 do it for the vibes: bustling square, street performances, snake charmers, and a feast for the senses. pro tip: bring some cash for photos with performers and for quick bites. best times: late afternoon into evening when the energy peaks. marrakesh morocco traveltiktok riads souks       discover the top 9 things to do in marrakesh, from staying in a riad to exploring the vibrant souks. a traveler’s dream awaits! morocco traveltiktok travelguide marrakech marrakech, morocco, is a vibrant city filled with rich history, stunning architecture, and unforgettable experiences. if you’re planning a trip and looking for the perfect itinerary, here’s a detailed 3-day plan to help you explore the best of marrakech.  **day 1: discover the historical landmarks**   start your journey at the remarkable **ben youssef madrasa**, an architectural gem showcasing intricately carved wooden doors and beautiful tile work. this former quranic school is a glimpse into morocco’s educational history. next, visit **dar el bacha**, which offers insight into the opulent life of the pashas in the past. don’t forget to explore **bahia palace**, a mid-19th century masterpiece with stunning gardens and enchanting courtyards, ideal for leisurely strolling.  **day 2: dive into the culture and markets**   on your second day, immerse yourself in the lively atmosphere of the **medina**. wander through the bustling souks, where you can find everything from spices to handcrafted jewelry. make sure to stop by **jemaa el-fnaa**, the main square, where you can experience street performances and savor local cuisine. if you have time, visit some local **museums** focused on moroccan art and culture, offering a deeper understanding of the region's past and present.  **day 3: adventure on the outskirts**   take a break from the urban experience with a **camel ride in the agafay desert**. this unique experience allows you to enjoy the serene desert landscape, making for great photo opportunities and memorable moments. in the evening, treat yourself to dinner at **dar moha**, where you can taste traditional moroccan dishes in an elegant setting, perfect for concluding your marrakech adventure.  make sure to save this itinerary for your next trip to marrakech! each day offers unique experiences that truly showcase the essence of this beautiful moroccan city.

straight past if we hadn’t kept digging. so if you’re planning a trip, here are the 4 spots we’d genuinely recommend in the medina in marrakech 👇 🌯 1. merguez, chicken & kefta sandwich 📍 chez omar – derb dabachi one of the city’s best-known sandwich shops. order the mixed sandwich loaded with chicken, spicy merguez sausage, kefta and grilled onions, all packed into fresh bread. 💰 20 dh / €2 🥙 2. chicken shawarma 📍 snack jad – 33 rue tachenbacht (short walk from jemaa el-fnaa). order the large with algerian sauce. the chicken is finished on the grill, giving it crispy edges while staying juicy inside. 💰 40 dh / €3.70 🍢 3. mixed skewers + white beans & lentils 📍 location pinned below ⬇️ this spot doesn’t really have a clear name, so we’ve pinned the exact location. the mixed skewers were some of the best we found, served with slow-cooked white beans and lentils, a classic accompaniment that’s perfect with fresh bread. 💰 90 dh / €8.45 total 📍 coordinates: 31.6307, -7.9866 tucked in an alley way where you’ll find other grilled skewer stands.  🍩 4. sfenj (moroccan donuts) 📍 boulabads – j2h8+4m6, rue sidi boulabada morocco’s famous doughnut. light, crispy and surprisingly savoury, so top things we wish we knew before visiting marrakech for the first time 🇲🇦 1. the official language is arabic but many people also speak french, english and spanish  2. if you are staying centrally in the medina then there is no need for any vehicles - you can easily get around on foot! many streets are actually too small for a car 🚶 3. you need to book tickets for jardin majorelle and the ysl museum in advance. this can be done online  4. if you’re short on time but still want to experience the desert, you can book an overnight tour to the agafay desert 🏜️  5. make sure you get an esim or local sim card on arrival, especially if you are visiting the medina! it can be very tricky to navigate  6. don’t be shy to bargain and barter - it’s expected and welcomed! 7. remember that you need to validate your boarding pass at the airport before flying home. this is on top of checking in online so make sure you leave extra time at the airport! ✈️ let us know thing else you wished you knew! ⬇️ marrakech visitmarrakech morocco visitmorocco miniguidetomarrakech  descubre lo que necesitas saber antes de tu primera visita a marrakech. ¡prepárate para una experiencia inolvidable! marrakech visitmarrakech morocco

2 days in marrakech, morocco 🇲🇦  in 60 seconds - culture, cats and nature jemaa el-fnaa square, souks, berber village, ourika valley, setti fatma waterfalls  morocco marrakech souk berber ourika  dive into marrakech, morocco for two days filled with culture, beautiful landscapes, and adorable cats! discover the gems of jemaa el-fnaa. morocco marrakech souk berber ourika marrakech, morocco is a vibrant city packed with culture, stunning natural landscapes, and charming feline companions. over the course of just two days, you can experience the essence of this incredible location.   on day 1, begin your adventure at jemaa el-fnaa square, the bustling heart of marrakech. here, the air is filled with the scents of spices and street food, while the sounds of performers and vendors create an electric atmosphere. visit the local souks to find unique treasures ranging from handcrafted goods to fragrant spices. don't forget to experience traditional henna applications and enjoy a horse and carriage ride through the enchanting streets. take in the sights of the historic kutubiyya mosque, and keep an eye out for the many friendly cats that roam around, adding to the city’s charm.  day 2 takes you to the stunning landscapes of ourika valley. this picturesque area offers a chance to immerse yourself in nature while visiting a traditional berber village. you'll have the opportunity to enjoy a scenic hike up to the breathtaking setti fatma waterfalls. along the way, see a demonstration of traditional stone carving and enjoy a delightful lunch by the riverside, tasting the flavors of moroccan cuisine.  marrakech is a city that beautifully combines the old with the new, providing memories that are sure to last a lifetime. so pack your bags and make the most of your visit in just two days, embracing the culture, nature, and the endearing cats that greet you along the way.

the souks for crafts and spices; bahia palace and saadian tombs for architecture; and a riad stay to experience traditional moroccan hospitality.  planning a sahara visit the sahara of morocco is best experienced on an overnight desert tour. typical options include 1–3 night trips from marrakesh that combine atlas mountain passes, berber villages, and a camel trek into the dunes. choose a tour that includes: pickup from marrakesh, sunset and sunrise time in the dunes, a camel ride, and overnight desert camping (traditional tents with dinner).  best time to travel avoid extreme heat in midsummer; they’re a common and memorable sight during the climb.  - midway stops: tours typically pause halfway for refreshments — enjoy a fresh drink while taking in the valley panorama.  - lunch with a view: many itineraries include a lunch overlooking the mountains and valley, a standout moment of the day.  - camel rides: on the return journey some operators offer camel rides, adding a fun, authentic-feeling finale to the trip.  practical tips - booking: you can easily book a guided tour from marrakech; look for trips labeled “ourika valley day trip” or “atlas mountains excursion.”  - fitness: trails range from easy viewpoints to steeper paths; choose according to your comfort and time.  - wildlife: admire monkeys from a distance and avoid feeding them.  - weather: bring layers — mountain temperatures can be cooler than marrakech.  - timing: a full-day schedule gives time for waterfalls, lunch, short hikes and photo stops without rushing.  why add ourika valley to your morocco itinerary the valley combines natural beauty and accessible adventure close to marrakech. highlights — waterfalls, mountain air, monkeys, scenic refreshment stops, a memorable lunch overlooking the valley and optional camel rides — create a compact, unforgettable day trip suitable for most travelers.  takeaway for anyone visiting marrakech, an ourika valley day trip into the atlas mountains is a must-see: straightforward to book, rich in scenery and experiences, and ideal for capturing morocco’s natural side. save this for your morocco itinerary and plan a day to enjoy waterfalls, mountain views, and local charm.  engagement prompt have you visited the ourika valley or taken a day trip from marrakech? share your favorite moment or ask for tour recommendations.

se perdre dans les souks, acheter des trésors impossibles à caser dans la valise, siroter des thés à volonté au coucher du soleil et créer des liens inattendus — voilà le programme idéal. ce guide réunit les idées et repères pour préparer un séjour entre filles à marrakech, en gardant le charme et l’ambiance évoqués : shopping, rencontres et moments partagés.  pourquoi marrakech est parfaite pour un girls trip - marchés et souks : flâner entre tissus, bijoux et objets artisanaux ouvre la porte à de belles trouvailles et à des moments complices. l’expérience de se perdre ensemble dans le souk fait partie du voyage. - shopping unique : articles faits main et pièces originales que l’on risque de « ne pas pouvoir mettre dans la valise » — souvenirs authentiques et cadeaux. - ambiance conviviale : salons de thé, rooftops et couchers de soleil pour des soirées partagées autour d’un thé à la menthe.  idées d’activités pour un séjour entre filles - balade dans la médina : prévoir des arrêts pour photos, pauses thé et négociations dans les boutiques. - session shopping coordonnée : cibler les ateliers de cuir, tapis et céramique pour des achats groupés ou des pièces à ramener. - rooftops et couchers de soleil : repérer 1–2 terrasses pour des soirées au coucher du soleil, parfaites pour créer des souvenirs. - salons de thé et cafés : tester le thé à la menthe à volonté et partager des moments de détente. - excursions courtes : si le groupe le souhaite, organiser une sortie d’une demi-journée (jardin, palais ou excursion dans l’atlas).  conseils pratiques - bagages : prévoir une valise souple et accepter que certains achats restent des souvenirs locaux si la taille ou le poids posent problème. - sécurité et surveillance : rester groupées dans les zones très touristiques et convenir de points de rendez-vous — « qui m’a laissé dans le souk sans surveillance ? » peut se transformer en anecdote si on s’organise. - budget et négociation : négocier avec bienveillance dans les souks et répartir les dépenses communes pour activités et taxis. - hébergement : privilégier un riad ou une maison d’hôtes avec espaces communs pour des soirées entre filles.  petit itinéraire suggéré (48–72 heures) - jour 1 : arrivée, installation au riad, première balade dans la médina et thé au coucher du soleil. - jour 2 : matinée souks et shopping, après-midi détente dans un salon de thé, soirée rooftop. - jour 3 : visite d’un jardin ou palais, achat de derniers souvenirs et départ.  conclusion un girls trip à marrakech promet shopping, thés à volonté, sunsets et amitiés renforcées. avec quelques repères pratiques — bagages, sécurité dans les souks et choix d’un hébergement convivial — le voyage devient une collection de moments précieux. prêtes à organiser votre getaway ? 🇲🇦🐪 morroco fyp souks travel marrakesh  explore marrakesh souks: top markets, what to buy, bargaining tips and travel highlights for morocco. practical advice for first-time visitors. morroco fyp souks travel marrakesh lead marrakesh souks are the heart of morocco’s iconic market culture. this guide highlights key souks in marrakesh, what to buy, bargaining tips and practical travel advice for first-time visitors exploring morocco’s markets.  body what the souks are: the souks in marrakesh are clustered marketplaces selling crafts, textiles, spices, leather, pottery, metalwork and more. walking through narrow alleys, visitors find specialized stalls grouped by product type.  top markets and areas to visit: - souk semmarine: central, good for textiles and general shopping. - souk ableuh and spice lanes: known for spices, dried fruit and culinary goods. - tannery areas and leather stalls: for leather bags, belts and jackets. - metalwork and lantern souks: traditional lamps, brass and copper pieces. - artisan workshops near jemaa el-fnaa: live demonstrations and handcrafted goods.  what to buy: - textiles: scarves, blankets, traditional fabrics. - leather goods: bags, slippers (babouches), belts. - homewares: lamps, pottery, metal trays. - spices and food items: saffron, cumin, preserved lemons. - souvenirs and crafts: hand-knotted rugs, jewelry, argan oil products.  bargaining and pricing tips: - prices are negotiable; start lower than the first offer and be polite but firm. - know a rough expected price for common items to avoid overpaying. - cash is preferred; carry small bills and change for better offers. - if unsure, walk away—sellers often call you back with a lower price.  practical travel advice: - wear comfortable shoes; alleys are uneven and busy. - keep valuables secure and be mindful of pickpocketing in crowded areas. - agree on price and inspect items before paying. - respect local customs: modest dress and courteous behavior go a long way. - consider visiting early morning or late afternoon to avoid peak crowds and heat.  closing / call to action explore marrakesh’s souks with an open mind for discovery and a few bargaining tricks. what traditional moroccan item are you most excited to buy? share your top souk finds or questions before your trip. morroco fyp souks travel marrakesh

spend the day with me in marrakesh 🇲🇦 dayvlog dayinmylife travel marrakech morocco  join me on my first day in marrakech as we explore culture, food, and stunning architecture. experience morocco's magic! dayvlog travel marrakech morocco join me on my exciting first day in marrakesh, morocco! 🇲🇦✨ after waking up to ten missed calls from my mom (who was eagerly waiting for breakfast!), i quickly washed up in my pajamas and headed down for a delicious fresh omelette. feeling ready for the day, i took in the stunning views of the old town, filled with charming shops offering food, souvenirs, and hand-painted portraits.  my goal for the day was to find a traditional moroccan kaftan, but sadly, i didn’t manage to find one. instead, my mom and i discovered a little mall. to our surprise, i stumbled upon a laura piano for just $30! while the quality wasn't top-notch, it was perfect for keeping warm since the weather turned out to be quite chilly. we decided to pick up some comfy uggs to stay cozy!  as we explored the mall, i was captivated by the abundance of exotic fruits and vibrant spices—everything looked so fresh and inviting! after our mini shopping spree, we hopped in a taxi to the yves saint laurent museum, which i was especially excited to see, as ysl was born in marrakesh. the museum was small, and photography wasn't allowed, but the beauty of the space made the visit worthwhile, even if i probably wouldn't return. we later relaxed at their café, though my mom was a bit disappointed with the size of our mojito compared to my coffee!  we continued our journey, heading to the historic madrasa ben youssef, the oldest islamic school in marrakesh. the entry ticket was just five dollars, and i was left in awe by the breathtaking architecture and serene atmosphere. it genuinely moved me, and i found myself tearing up at the stunning beauty all around.  as night fell, we made our way back to our accommodation, fascinated by the little door that led to a surprisingly expansive space inside. when we arrived, we were greeted with a delightful surprise of rose petals scattered about! after changing into something more comfortable and warm, we set off for dinner, navigating the bustling streets under the starry sky.  we finally found our restaurant, boasting a remarkable 4.9-star rating with thousands of reviews! we indulged in two iconic moroccan dishes: couscous and tangia. my love for moroccan cuisine continues to grow! 😍 after dinner, we took a wrong turn and ended up needing some help to find our way home, but eventually, we made it back around 9 pm. exhausted but happy, i was ready to shower and relax after such a fulfilling day.  thanks for joining me on this adventure in marrakesh! can’t wait to share more of my journey with you all! 🙌💕   dayinmylife travel marrakech morocco culinaryadventure culturalexperience architecturelovers momanddaughtertime

it becomes livelier at night with more food and entertainment.   - quick cultural stops: courtyards of riads, a short visit to a historic mosque exterior (non-muslims may not enter certain mosques), and public gardens for a calm break.    safety and comfort for solo travelers - stay aware of surroundings and set simple boundaries with persistent vendors. polite but firm responses work best.   - hydration and heat: carry water and wear comfortable shoes for walking on uneven streets.   - local customs: dress respectfully — lightweight coverage helps when visiting religious or traditional areas.    practical gear to carry on day one - small lockable bag, photocopy of passport, cash in dirhams, phone with local sim or roaming, portable charger, sunscreen, and a map app preloaded offline.    closing / takeaway your first day in morocco—especially marrakech—should be about settling in, gentle exploration, and absorbing the atmosphere. keep plans flexible, prioritize safety and comfort, and let the medina’s energy guide your next days of adventure. where will you explore first in marrakech?

replying to @ss is marrakech safe for women traveling alone? it depends on your expectations of safety. more from morocco:  @stacy hughes  . . girlswhotravel marrakech travelsafety morocco mynextadventure  real-world advice on whether marrakech is safe for solo female travelers, what to expect, and practical safety tips. girlswhotravel marrakech travelsafety morocco mynextadventure is marrakech safe for women traveling alone? practical safety tips  short answer safety in marrakech for women traveling alone depends on expectations and personal precautions. many solo female travelers visit marrakech without major incidents, but being prepared and aware of local customs improves comfort and reduces risk.  what to expect - street harassment and persistent vendors: in busy medina areas expect frequent attention from shopkeepers, guides, and vendors. most approaches are persistent rather than violent; clear, polite refusals usually work. carrying a polite but firm script helps. - crowds and chaotic traffic: jemaa el-fnaa and souks are crowded and lively. watch your belongings and plan routes to avoid getting jostled in busy alleys. - cultural norms: morocco is a conservative country. dressing modestly (covering shoulders and knees) can reduce unwanted attention and shows cultural respect. - night safety: stick to well-lit, populated streets and use trusted transport for late returns. many guests feel safest returning to their riad or hotel after dark.  practical safety tips - choose accommodations wisely: pick a reputable riad or hotel in a safe neighborhood with good reviews from solo female travelers. - share your plans: leave arrival times and daily plans with someone at home or a contact in morocco. - use trusted transport: pre-arranged taxis from your riad or reputable ride apps reduce risk; agree on fares beforehand if meters aren’t used. - stay aware of belongings: keep valuables secure, use a crossbody bag, and avoid showing large amounts of cash or expensive jewelry. - learn basic phrases: a few arabic or french phrases and a polite tone can ease interactions and help defuse persistent approaches. - set boundaries: use a firm “non, merci” and walk away when sellers or guides won’t take no for an answer. if you feel unsafe, seek nearby shops, hotels, or police. - dress to blend in: modest clothing can lower the chance of harassment; lightweight layers work well in marrakech’s climate.  when to be extra cautious - late-night solo walks in less tourist areas - isolated alleyways or quiet streets far from main squares - accepting unsolicited offers of help or “free” guided tours  resources and community - read recent reviews from other solo female travelers for up-to-date experiences - follow travel creators who report from morocco for realistic expectations - use local expatriate groups or women-traveler forums for advice on neighborhoods and riads  takeaway marrakech can be safe for women traveling alone when expectations are realistic and basic precautions are followed: choose reputable lodging, respect local customs, stay aware in crowds, and use trusted transport. many women travel successfully and enjoy marrakech’s markets, food, and culture while staying vigilant.  would you like a short packing checklist or recommended riad search tips for solo female travelers to marrakech?

food cowfeet mukbang exotic lead discover morocco's cow feet dish through flavor notes and serving style. this article explains what cow feet (cow trotters) are in moroccan cuisine, how they fit into traditional meals, and what to expect during a mukbang-style tasting.  what is cow feet in moroccan food cow feet, also called cow trotters, are featured in moroccan cooking as a slow-cooked, gelatin-rich dish. prepared with spices, aromatics, and long simmering, the meat and connective tissue break down into a tender, flavorful stew often served with bread or rice.  traditional preparation and flavors typical moroccan seasonings pair well with cow feet: garlic, onion, cumin, coriander, paprika, and sometimes ginger or saffron. the dish is simmered slowly so the collagen becomes gelatinous, creating a soft texture and rich mouthfeel. broth is usually spiced and sometimes finished with fresh herbs or a squeeze of lemon.  how it's served and eaten cow feet are commonly served family-style. diners use bread to soak up the broth or eat with rice. expect a communal presentation where pieces are pulled apart by hand or with simple utensils. in a mukbang context, the focus is on texture, savory intensity, and the social experience of tasting an exotic moroccan food.  mukbang tasting notes during a mukbang, emphasis is on sensory reaction: the tenderness of the meat, the richness of the broth, and the balance of spices. reactions often highlight the gelatinous texture, savory depth, and how the flavors compare to other moroccan favorites.  why try morocco's cow feet trying cow feet offers a window into traditional moroccan cooking techniques—slow simmering, layering spices, and communal eating. it's an example of nose-to-tail cooking where every part of the animal is valued and transformed into flavorful dishes.  takeaway and engagement if you're curious about authentic moroccan flavors or adventurous foods, cow feet are worth trying for their mi primer viaje a marruecos!! viaje marruecos morocco marrakech  relato y consejos de mi primer viaje a marruecos: qué ver en marrakech, comidas, mercados y recomendaciones prácticas. viaje marruecos morocco marrakech mi primer viaje a marruecos: guía y recuerdos de marrakech  inicio — la experiencia esencial mi primer viaje a marruecos fue una mezcla de olores, colores y sonidos: las calles de marrakech, los zocos y la plaza jemaa el-fna quedaron grabados como los momentos más intensos del viaje. aquí comparto lo vivido y las recomendaciones prácticas para quien planee una visita similar.  qué ver en marrakech - plaza jemaa el-fna: centro vivo de la ciudad donde los espectáculos callejeros, puestos de comida y vendedores se agrupan al atardecer. ideal para sentir la energía local. - zocos (mercados): recorrer los pasillos de artesanía, telas, especias y cerámica. negociar precios es habitual; empezar ofreciendo un 30–50% menos y acercarse al acuerdo. - medersa y riads: visitar una madraza o quedarse en un riad tradicional para experimentar la arquitectura y la hospitalidad marroquí. - jardines y palacios: si el tiempo lo permite, incluir jardines como el majorelle y palacios para ver contraste entre la vida urbana y espacios tranquilos.  comida y bebida - probar tajine, cuscús, pastilla y té de menta: sabores emblemáticos y abundantes en puestos y restaurantes. - precauciones: elegir puestos con buena afluencia para minimizar riesgos estomacales; beber agua embotellada si se busca mayor seguridad.  consejos prácticos de viaje - ropa y respeto cultural: vestir de forma respetuosa en zonas tradicionales; llevar una bufanda ligera para protegerse del sol o del polvo. - regateo y pagos: el regateo es parte de la experiencia en los zocos; llevar efectivo en moneda local para pequeños pagos. - transporte: caminar por la medina y usar taxis para trayectos más largos; acordar tarifa con el conductor antes de subir cuando sea posible. - seguridad: marrakech es generalmente segura para turistas, pero mantener objetos de valor controlados y estar atento en zonas muy concurridas.  itinerario sugerido (breve) - día 1: llegada, paseo por la medina y plaza jemaa el-fna al atardecer. - día 2: zocos y compras, visita a un riad y cena con comida local. - día 3: jardines y palacios, tiempo libre y preparación de regreso.  cierre — últimas impresiones este primer viaje a marruecos, centrado en marrakech, dejó recuerdos duraderos: la combinación de sabores, la artesanía y la vida en la plaza principal. para quien viaje por primera vez, combinar recorridos a pie con momentos de descanso en riads ofrece un equilibrio ideal entre descubrimiento y comodidad.  ¿planeas tu primer viaje a marruecos? comparte qué te gustaría ver y puedo añadir recomendaciones específicas para tus intereses.

travelawareness  scam  juice please watch my other tiktok videos showing the beauty of morocco and how i encourage people to visit this amazing country 🇲🇦✅ learn how the marrakech “waiting game” scam works and how to avoid being deceived—stay safe while enjoying morocco. marrakech morocco travelawareness scam juice why this matters: a traveler’s warning about the “waiting game” scam in marrakech.  i love morocco—the people, the food, and the vibes are fantastic. i visited over seven cities and posted more than 70 videos celebrating this country’s beauty. still, it’s important to share raw experiences so others can stay safe. during my trip i encountered only two negative incidents; this was one of them. it wasn’t about the money—it was the deceit.  what is the “waiting game” scam? the scam works by making you wait for no legitimate reason, often after a short interaction (a proposed ride, a purchase, or a service). the scammers stall, hoping you’ll walk away in frustration. if you do, they claim you left money behind and then split whatever cash they can extract from you. the core tactic is deceit and manipulation rather than a direct demand for money.  where it happens this tactic can appear in busy tourist places such as market stalls, taxi stands, or near attractions in marrakech and other moroccan cities. the scenario often looks normal at first—a friendly offer, a quick arrangement—then turns into unnecessary delays and pressure.  how to recognize it - unnecessary stalling: prolonged waiting without clear reason or updates.   - group coordination: multiple people suddenly involved in the interaction.   - pressure to stay or leave with them: suggestions that you can’t safely go on your own.   - claims after you leave: accusations that you abandoned money or items when you hadn’t.  how to avoid it - stay calm and clear: if waiting feels unnatural, ask direct questions and request a clear timeline.   - keep belongings close: never leave cash or valuables unattended while distracted.   - decline grouped offers: prefer official taxis, reputable guides, or established shops.   - walk away confidently: if you feel uneasy, leave the situation without rushing or showing frustration.   - use witnesses: involve other tourists, staff, or police if the situation escalates.    why i’m sharing this the goal is awareness, not fear. morocco is an amazing country with warm people and incredible experiences. sharing this incident is meant to help travelers enjoy their trip while avoiding a common deceitful tactic.  final takeaway enjoy morocco’s beauty, but stay alert to subtle scams like the “waiting game.” trust your instincts, protect your belongings, and prefer clear, official services when possible. if you’ve experienced something similar, share your story to help others stay safe and keep the travel community informed.  call to action watch more of my videos showing morocco’s beauty and helpful travel tips, and share this warning to keep fellow travelers safe. marrakech morocco travelawareness scam juice

essaouira is the perfect escape with its beautiful madinah and fresh seafood. easy day trip from marrakech or agadir, deffo worth it 🇲🇦🏝️travel travelvlog traveltok 🇲🇦🇩🇿🇹🇳🇱🇾 المغرب  discover essaouira, morocco's underrated coastal gem. explore its beautiful medina, enjoy fresh seafood, and find the best day trip from marrakech & agadir. travel morocco essaouira, morocco, offers a perfect escape with its stunning medina and delicious fresh seafood, making it a worthwhile day trip from marrakech or agadir.  explore the top seven activities to experience in this captivating coastal city:  1.  **walk along the coastline:** immerse yourself in the tranquil beauty of essaouira's coastline, characterized by desert landscapes, the ocean, dramatic cliffs, and vast open spaces. this is a truly relaxing experience.  2.  **camel ride on the beach:** enhance your coastal experience with a camel ride along the picturesque beach, offering breathtaking views of the beautiful shoreline.  3.  **explore local markets:** dive into the vibrant atmosphere of essaouira's local markets. discover a wide array of food, clothing, and goods, and get lost in the maze of streets within the market.  4.  **visit the city walls:** ascend to the historic city walls, a significant tourist attraction, and take in the unreal, panoramic views from atop.  5.  **experience the fishing pool:** for a unique culinary adventure, visit the fishing pool. here, you can select fresh fish directly from the markets and have them cooked for you at adjacent seafood restaurants.  6.  **quad biking at sunset:** embark on an exhilarating quad biking adventure, especially during the magical sunset hours. this activity is highly recommended for an unforgettable experience, costing around £20.  essaouira is an underrated destination offering a blend of relaxation, adventure, and cultural immersion.

welcome to morocco 🇲🇦✈️ planning morocco? discover top tours from marrakech, must-see sights, and travel tips for first-time visitors. morocco marrakech travel welcome to morocco — essential travel information for first-time visitors and anyone planning tours from marrakech.  quick overview - main focus: tours and day trips from marrakech and a short marrakech city guide. - who this helps: first-time visitors, travelers flying into morocco, and people choosing excursions from marrakech.  why base in marrakech marrakech is a central hub for exploring morocco. the city offers direct access to popular day trips, guided tours, and transport links to other regions. staying in marrakech lets you combine a compact city experience with easy departures for desert trips, coastal towns, and atlas mountain excursions.  top tours from marrakech - essaouira day trip: coastal town known for beaches, seafood, and a relaxed medina. ideal for a full-day escape from marrakech. - ourika valley and atlas mountains: short drive into the foothills for scenic views, waterfalls, and local berber villages. - agafay desert & sunset tours: closer than the sahara for a desert-like landscape, popular for short outings and sunset photography. - sahara desert multi-day tours: organized trips east toward merzouga/dunas for overnight camel treks and desert camps (requires 2–3 days minimum). - ouzoud waterfalls: one of morocco’s highest waterfalls; a popular nature day trip for hiking and picnic stops.  marrakech city guide — must-see spots - jemaa el-fnaa: the famous central square with food stalls, storytellers, and evening energy. - medina souks: narrow alleys for shopping textiles, spices, leather goods, and handicrafts. - majorelle garden & yves saint laurent museum: calm gardens and fashion history near the city center. - koutoubia mosque: iconic mosque and landmark visible from many parts of the city. - bahia palace & saadian tombs: historical sites showcasing moroccan architecture.  practical travel tips - best time to visit: spring and autumn for milder temperatures; summers can be very hot inland. - currency and payments: moroccan dirham (mad). cash is common in markets; cards work at hotels and larger shops. - local customs: dress modestly in public spaces; polite greetings and bargaining are normal in souks. - transportation: marrakech has taxis, buses, and organized tours. for multi-day trips, consider booked tour operators for convenience. - safety and health: standard travel precautions apply. stay hydrated, protect from sun, and use reputable tour operators for desert treks.  how to choose a tour - length: day trips are best for nearby highlights; reserve multi-day tours for distant destinations like the sahara. - reviews: look for operators with consistent positive feedback and clear itineraries. - inclusions: check whether transport, guides, entrance fees, and meals are included.  closing and next steps marrakech is a practical base to explore morocco’s variety — from vibrant medina life to mountain valleys and desert landscapes. decide which day trips or multi-day excursions match your schedule, read reviews, and book in advance for peak seasons. ready to plan your marrakech-based itinerary? which destination interests you most: essaouira, the atlas mountains, or the sahara?

أنواع الإيجار المتاحة: فيلا مع مسبح خاص، فيلا فاخرة مع طباخ أو طاقم، فيلا عائلية حتى 10 أشخاص، شقق مفروشة قصيرة المدة أو للإقامة السياحية. - مواقع مميزة: قرب جامع الفنا، حي جيليز، النخيل، ومناطق سياحية أخرى. - مدد الإيجار: يومي، قصير المدى، عطلات، وإيجار طويل.  أنواع العقارات ووصفها - فيلا بمسبح خاص: مناسبة للعائلات أو مجموعات حتى 10 أشخاص، توفر خصوصية ومساحة خارجية للترفيه. - فيلا فاخرة: تجهيزات أعلى، خدمة طباخ أو طاقم متاحة عند الطلب، ملائمة للمناسبات أو إقامة مريحة. - شقة مفروشة: خيار اقتصادي للعطلات أو الإقامات القصيرة، متوفرة في مراكز المدينة وأحياء مثل جيليز والنخيل. - إقامة سياحية / riad: بديل تقليدي مراكشي لتجربة محلية أصيلة.  كيف تختار الأنسب 1. حدد الغرض: عطلة قصيرة أم إقامة عائلية طويلة؟ 2. عدد النزلاء: اختر فيلا عائلية أو شقة بحسب السعة (بعض الفيلات تستوعب حتى 10 أشخاص). 3. الميزانية: شقق رخيصة متاحة، وفيلات فاخرة بأسعار أعلى مقابل الخصوصية والخدمات. 4. الموقع: قرب جامع الفنا لسهولة الوصول إلى المعالم، جيليز للتسوق والمعيشة العصرية. 5. الخدمات: هل تحتاج مسبح خاص، طباخ، أو طاقم خدمة؟  نصائح عملية للحجز - تحقق من تقييمات الضيوف وصور حقيقة للعقار. - اسأل عن سياسة الدفع والإلغاء والرسوم الإضافية (تنظيف، طاقة، ضيافة). - استعلم عن توفر الخدمات (واي فاي، موقف سيارات، تكييف، خدمة طباخ). - للمجموعات الكبيرة احجز مبكرًا خاصة في مواسم الذروة.  خاتمة مراكش تقدم خيارات واسعة من كراء الشقق والفلل: من شقق مفروشة اقتصادية إلى فيلات فاخرة بمسبح خاص وخدمات شخصية. حدد احتياجك من حيث السعة والموقع والخدمات ثم اختر الأنسب لرحلتك.  هل تحتاج قائمة عقارات أو نصائح للحجز عبر airbnb أو وكالات محلية؟ اذكر المدينة والميزانية لمساعدة أكثر تخصيصًا.

those camels were on their worst behaviour 🤣 camelriding agafaydesert marrakech  real pov of camel riding in agafay desert near marrakech — rude camels, photo tips, and what to expect on a desert ride. camelriding agafaydesert marrakech lead a pov camel ride in the agafay desert near marrakech where the camels were notably unruly. this quick guide explains what to expect on an agafay camel ride, how to lock in for pictures, and tips for handling difficult camel behaviour during a short desert excursion.  what to expect on an agafay desert camel ride - duration and setting: most camel rides in agafay are short excursions across rocky, moonlike terrain rather than long sahara treks. expect open views, low dunes, and a scenic backdrop for photos. - animal behaviour: camels can be unpredictable. the featured ride shows camels on their worst behaviour — shifting, vocalizing, or resisting poses. guides usually manage the animals, but riders may need to steady themselves. - photo moments: the cover sticker — “pov: you have to lock in for the pictures” — highlights that good photos require concentration. hold your pose, keep your balance, and follow the guide’s timing for the best shots.  practical tips for a smoother camel ride - listen to your guide: follow instructions for mounting, dismounting, and positioning for photos. - secure your belongings: keep cameras, phones, and loose items fastened so they don’t fall during sudden camel movements. - body position: sit upright, keep feet steady in the stirrups or footrests, and hold the saddle handle or rope lightly but firmly. - stay calm: camels react to sudden movements and noise. quiet, steady behavior helps reduce unexpected actions.  getting the best photos - lock in your expression and posture when the guide signals — many memorable frames come from a few coordinated seconds. - use a strap for your phone or camera and set a fast shutter to avoid motion blur. - capture the environment: wide shots of the agafay landscape give context beyond close-ups of the camel.  safety and respect - treat animals respectfully: avoid provoking or teasing the camels. follow local operator rules. - choose reputable operators that care for animal welfare and provide experienced handlers.  closing a camel ride in agafay desert near marrakech can be a fun, photo-ready experience even when the camels misbehave. expect short scenic rides, be prepared to steady yourself for photos, and follow your guide’s instructions for a safe and memorable outing. would you lock in for the picture or laugh along with the camels?

quick overview of marrakech, morocco — top sights, neighborhoods, and travel tips for first-time visitors. comewalkwithakhi marrakech lead a compact overview of marrakech, morocco focused on the city’s top sights, neighborhoods, and practical tips for first-time visitors. ideal for anyone planning a short visit or wanting a quick orientation to what makes marrakech distinctive.  top highlights - jemaa el-fnaa: the central square and performance heart of marrakech where street food stalls, storytellers, and musicians gather, especially lively at dusk. - medina (old city): a tangle of alleys filled with souks selling textiles, spices, leather goods and pottery; expect bargaining and narrow passages. - koutoubia mosque: marrakech’s landmark mosque visible from many points in the city; notable for its minaret and surrounding gardens. - bahia palace and saadian tombs: two historic sites showcasing moroccan architecture, carved wood, and tilework. - majorelle garden & yves saint laurent museum: a tranquil botanical garden with vivid blue buildings and a museum celebrating the designer’s connection to marrakech.  neighborhoods to know - medina: historic core — best for souks, street life, and traditional riads (guesthouses). - gueliz (ville nouvelle): the modern district with shops, cafes, and wider streets; easier to navigate and offers contemporary amenities. - hivernage: upscale area near nightlife, hotels, and restaurants.  practical travel tips - best time to visit: spring (march–may) and autumn (september–november) for milder weather. - dress: respectful, modest clothing recommended when visiting religious sites and local neighborhoods. - money and bargaining: cash (moroccan dirham) is preferred in markets; polite bargaining is expected in souks. - getting around: walk the medina where possible; taxis are affordable for longer trips—agree on a price or ensure the meter is used. - accommodation: stay in a riad in the medina for an authentic experience or choose hotels in gueliz for modern conveniences.  food and dining - street food: try local dishes at jemaa el-fnaa stalls — tagine, couscous, and harira soup are local staples. - cafes and restaurants: explore both medina terraces and gueliz bistros for a range of moroccan and international options.  what to expect - sensory experience: busy markets, fragrant spice stalls, ornate craftsmanship, and both chaotic and charming street life. - cultural etiquette: greet with a smile; ask before photographing people; be prepared for friendly haggling in markets.  close / call-to-action marrakech offers a compact, sensory-rich overview of moroccan culture in one city — from the bustle of jemaa el-fnaa to serene gardens and historic palaces. which part of marrakech would you explore first: the medina souks, majorelle garden, or the historic sites?

these are the top tips to know before you go—practical advice and must-see ideas for first-time visitors looking to make the most of beaches, day trips, and island transport.  getting around 1. take ubers (and taxis) when needed: rideshare and taxi services can save time between towns and are convenient for early-morning or late-night transfers. keep local payment methods ready and check estimated fares before booking. 2. public transport: buses cover many routes on the main island but can be slow or irregular during peak season. combine buses with rideshares for flexibility.  where to explore 1. explore outside valletta: valletta is a highlight, but nearby towns and villages show malta’s character—historic streets, local cafes, and quieter beaches. plan time in mdina, marsaxlokk, and small harbor towns. 2. perfect day trip from sicily: malta makes an excellent day or overnight trip from sicily. ferries run between sicily and malta; consider a day trip focused on coastal sights or an overnight to explore more inland.  beaches and sea sights 1. blue grotto, not blue lagoon: for dramatic sea caves and coastal photography, prioritize blue grotto on the main island. the blue lagoon (on comino) is famous but can be crowded—choose based on the experience you want. 2. maltese beaches: research each beach’s access and facilities—some are sandy, others rocky. pack sun protection and water shoes for rocky shores.  practical tips 1. save this before your euro summer: bookmark planning notes—transport apps, ferry schedules, and site opening hours—to avoid surprises during peak season. 2. local customs and timing: meals and site hours can differ from home; check opening times and local holiday schedules.  close / cta use these malta travel tips to shape an itinerary that fits your pace—whether it’s island hopping from sicily, hunting coastal caves at blue grotto, or exploring beyond valletta. which tip will you use first on your trip?

everything we did & how much it costs to road trip to sahara desert 🇲🇦🌙 it’s day 2-3 in morocco and we decided to take the 10 hour drive to the sahara desert. we made some mistakes along the way so make sure to learn from us!  tips for driving in morocco: - avoid driving at night. morocco has animals crossing the road, not many street lights, and we even ran into a few potholes.  - be very cautious of speed signs because moroccan police officers hide to catch people who are speeding.  - moroccan police officers also have random traffic stops in different cities  - so always drive with your international drivers license, passport, and car registration in case you get pulled over - split up your drive to the desert and get a hotel along the way - book your rental car with @discover cars  make sure to follow us so you don’t miss day 4 ✨ hi, we’re brennan and ashley :) a married dink couple navigating our 30s! if you like traveling, weekend getaways, and couple content, welcome! morocco saharadesert moroccoitinerary travelguide couplestravel detailed breakdown of what we did, mistakes we made, and costs for the 10-hour morocco drive to the sahara desert. practical driving tips and planning advice. morocco saharadesert travelguide lead a detailed account of day 2–3 of a morocco trip: a 10-hour road trip to the sahara desert, what we did, how much it cost, mistakes we made, and practical tips for driving and planning so you can avoid the same problems.  what we did and the route - drove roughly 10 hours from our starting point to reach the sahara desert area.  - split the journey over two days to avoid fatigue and to reduce risk when driving at night.  - booked a rental car (we used discover cars).  - stayed overnight at a hotel along the way to break up the long drive before reaching the desert.  costs (what to expect) - rental car: booked through discover cars (prices vary by season and vehicle type). expect typical rental costs plus insurance, fuel, and possible extras.  - fuel: plan for multiple fill-ups for a 10-hour drive across varied terrain.  - accommodation: budget for at least one overnight hotel when splitting the drive.  - food and incidentals: snacks, meals en route, and any small charges like parking or roadside stops.  note: exact numbers depend on travel dates, vehicle, and personal choices.  mistakes we made (learn from us) - attempted to cover too much distance without splitting the drive early enough.  - drove during low-light conditions where animals, potholes, and limited lighting increase risk.  - underestimated local traffic stops and enforcement of speed limits.  essential driving tips for morocco - avoid driving at night: animals frequently cross roads, street lighting is sparse, and there are potholes.  - respect speed signs: police often hide to catch speeding drivers.  - expect random traffic stops by moroccan police in different cities; always be prepared.  - carry documents: international driver’s license, passport, and car registration should be readily available if pulled over.  - split the drive: schedule at least one hotel stop en route to the sahara to rest and reset for safer daytime driving.  - book rental cars in advance: use reputable platforms (we used discover cars) to compare vehicles and insurance options.  planning checklist before you go - confirm rental car booking and insurance coverage.  - print or have digital copies of passport, international driver’s license, and car registration.  - plan daily driving distances and identify towns or hotels to stop at before nightfall.  - check local speed limits and road conditions for your planned route.  - budget for fuel, accommodation, food, and incidental expenses.  closing / takeaway road tripping to the sahara is a memorable adventure but requires planning: split long drives, avoid night driving, carry proper documents, and respect local traffic rules. booking a reliable rental and planning an overnight stop will make the 10-hour journey safer and more enjoyable. follow along for day 4 to see the rest of our morocco travel and more tips from brennan and ashley — a married dink couple exploring weekend getaways and travel in their 30s.  engagement prompt have you driven long distances in morocco or visited the sahara? share your tips or questions about planning a safe road trip below.

everything you need to know before going to monaco! hope you enjoy & find this helpful🫶🏼 follow for the next episode of our france travel diaries!🥂 monaco southoffrance frenchriviera monacoitinerary traveldiaries  complete monaco itinerary and travel tips for the french riviera — what to see, where to go, and practical advice before visiting monaco. monaco southoffrance frenchriviera traveldiaries lead planning a trip to monaco? this concise monaco itinerary and travel guide covers everything you need to know before you go: top sights, practical tips, and how to fit monaco into a south of france trip.  what to expect in monaco monaco is a compact, glamorous principality on the french riviera. expect upscale neighborhoods, luxury yachts in the harbor, historic monaco-ville (the old town), and the famous monte carlo district with its casino and grand hotels. the principality is walkable but built on steep hills; comfortable shoes are recommended.  top sights to include in your monaco itinerary - monaco-ville (old town): explore narrow streets, the prince’s palace exterior, and views over the harbor. great for photos and a short history stroll. - monte carlo casino: iconic façade and elegant surroundings. visiting the interior may require dress codes and entrance fees. - port hercules and larvotto beach: walk the marina to see yachts and enjoy the waterfront; larvotto offers a pebble beach and seaside cafés. - oceanographic museum: noted for marine exhibits and dramatic cliffside views. - exotic garden (jardin exotique): unusual plants and panoramic views of monaco and the mediterranean.  timing and itinerary suggestions - day trip: pick 2–3 highlights — monaco-ville, monte carlo, and a harbor walk or museum. arrival by train from nice or by car is straightforward. - half day: focus on the old town and a short harbor stroll; skip museums if time is limited. - overnight stay: enjoy evening dining in monte carlo, a relaxed morning at the gardens or museum, and a seaside walk before departing.  practical travel tips - getting there: trains from nice and other riviera towns are frequent and convenient. driving is possible but parking can be costly and limited. - walking: expect steep hills and steps; comfortable shoes are essential. - budget: monaco is expensive — plan for higher prices at restaurants, hotels, and attractions. - currency and border: euro is used; monaco sits on the french riviera and is easily combined with nearby french towns. - best time to visit: spring and early autumn offer pleasant weather and fewer crowds than mid-summer.  safety and etiquette monaco is generally safe and tourist-friendly. dress smartly if visiting upscale venues like the casino. be respectful of local rules in museums and public spaces.  closing / call to action use this monaco itinerary to plan your visit to the french riviera: choose a day trip or an overnight stay based on your pace, prioritize the old town and monte carlo, and prepare for an upscale experience with great views. follow for more france travel diary episodes and share which monaco spot you want to visit next.

ourika falls in morocco 🇲🇦  fyp marrakech🇲🇦morocco morocco traveltiktok girlstrip  visit ourika falls near marrakech: how to get there, what to expect, and tips for a girls' trip. marrakech🇲🇦morocco morocco traveltiktok girlstrip lead ourika falls, located in the ourika valley south of marrakech, is a popular day‑trip destination for travelers and especially for groups planning a girls’ trip. the site combines accessible waterfalls, short hikes, local cafés, and mountain scenery typical of rural morocco.  what to expect - scenery: terraced hills, river pools and cascading waterfalls. the main falls are the easiest to reach from the village and provide classic photo opportunities.  - difficulty: trails range from flat riverside walks to steeper footpaths and stone steps. basic fitness is sufficient for the main viewpoint; longer hikes require sturdier shoes.  - facilities: small cafés and stalls in the village sell refreshments and local snacks. expect limited formal facilities higher up the trails.  getting there - from marrakech: ourika valley is a short drive south of marrakech. travelers commonly reach the falls by private car, taxi, organized tour, or hired driver for a half‑day or full‑day trip.  - timing: morning visits avoid the midday crowds and heat. plan extra time for stops at viewpoints or local markets.  practical tips - footwear: wear comfortable walking shoes with good grip for uneven paths.  - clothing: respect local customs — dress modestly in village areas; light layers are useful for variable mountain weather.  - money: carry small bills for entrance fees, parking, tips, and purchases at cafés or stalls.  - safety: stay on marked paths and be cautious near wet rocks and river edges.  - guides: hiring a local guide can enrich the visit with cultural context and help navigate higher trails.  why go on a girls’ trip ourika falls offers a relaxed mix of nature, easy adventure, and photo spots that work well for groups. the short travel time from marrakech makes it convenient for an informal outing without an overnight stay.  closing / call to action plan a morning departure from marrakech, bring water and good shoes, and enjoy the waterfalls, river views, and village atmosphere of ourika valley. would you prefer a guided tour or a self‑drive visit to ourika falls on your next morocco trip?

📍jemaa el-fna – marrakech  véritable cœur de la médina, jemaa el-fna est la place la plus célèbre de marrakech et un incontournable lors de votre séjour. on y retrouve une ambiance unique qui change du matin au soir. ✨ sur place, tu trouveras : 🥤 des stands de jus de fruits frais 🍢 de nombreux stands de street food marocaine 🛍️ des boutiques et artisans locaux (épices, babouches, lanternes, souvenirs…) 🎶 des musiciens, danseurs et artistes de rue 🎨 des tatoueuses au henné 🐍 des charmeurs de serpents 🐒 des montreurs de singes 🐎 des calèches pour découvrir la ville 🕌 de nombreux cafés et rooftops avec vue sur la place 📍 située au cœur de la médina 💰 accès libre et gratuit 🌅 l’ambiance est particulièrement animée en fin de journée et en soirée. découvrez jemaa el-fna à marrakech : stands de jus, street food, artisans, musiciens, tatoueuses au henné et rooftop. conseils pour profiter de la place. marrakech jemaaelfna jemaa el-fna : le cœur vivant de la médina de marrakech  présentation rapide jemaa el-fna est la place la plus célèbre de marrakech, située au centre de la médina. l’accès est libre et gratuit. sa vie et son ambiance varient nettement du matin au soir, ce qui en fait un incontournable lors d’un séjour dans la ville.  ce que l’on trouve sur place - stands de jus de fruits frais : rafraîchissements naturels disponibles toute la journée. idéal pour une pause hydratation entre visites. - street food marocaine : brochettes, tajines rapides, beignets et spécialités locales à déguster sur le pouce. - boutiques et artisans locaux : épices, babouches, lanternes et souvenirs traditionnels vendus par des commerçants de la médina. - musiciens, danseurs et artistes de rue : spectacles gratuits qui animent la place, particulièrement en fin d’après-midi et en soirée. - tatoueuses au henné : motifs temporaires pour touristes et locaux, méthode traditionnelle. - charmeurs de serpents et montreurs de singes : attractions typiques de la place, à observer en gardant une distance respectueuse. - calèches : possibilité de faire un tour en calèche pour découvrir les environs. - cafés et rooftops : de nombreux établissements offrent des vues sur la place, parfaits pour contempler l’animation au coucher du soleil.  conseils pratiques - meilleur moment pour visiter : fin d’après-midi et soirée pour profiter de l’animation et des spectacles. - sécurité et respect : garder ses effets personnels proches dans les zones très animées. respecter les artistes et les animaux ; refuser poliment si vous ne souhaitez pas prendre de photos payantes. - négociation : il est courant de marchander dans les boutiques et pour certains services ; rester poli et fixer un prix avant d’acheter. - hygiène alimentaire : privilégier les stands fréquentés par du monde pour réduire les risques liés à l’alimentation de rue.  à retenir jemaa el-fna est une expérience multisensorielle : sons, odeurs, couleurs et saveurs se mêlent pour offrir une immersion authentique dans la culture marocaine. visiter la place le soir et monter sur un rooftop pour voir l’ensemble sont deux conseils simples pour tirer le meilleur parti de ce lieu emblématique.  envie d’y aller ? quels stands ou spectacles vous attirent le plus lors d’une visite à jemaa el-fna ?

if you’re only staying in marrakesh, you’re missing half of morocco. we booked a 3-day imperial cities tour and honestly, it changed the whole trip. day one started in casablanca at the hassan ii mosque! one of the largest mosques in the world and the only one in morocco open to non-muslims. what makes it even more special? every moroccan citizen contributed just $2 toward building it. it sits right on the atlantic ocean and the architecture is genuinely breathtaking. from there we drove to rabat, morocco’s capital city, which most tourists completely skip. we walked through the portuguese and jewish historic neighbourhoods, visited the beaches and soaked in centuries of history. our guide arranged everything! we even had free time to explore and shop. we ended the day arriving in fez, checked into our riad and sat down for dinner. accommodation, breakfast and dinner all pre-arranged. genuinely stress free. day two was a full five-hour guided tour through the fez medina, the oldest medieval city in the world, a unesco world heritage site with over 9,000 streets. we visited a traditional tannery where leather has been dyed the same way for over 1,000 years, met master craftsmen and had the chance to commission custom furniture, carpets and ceramics. moroccan craftsmanship is on another level. we also visited al-qarawiyyin — the first university ever founded in the world, built in 859 ce by fatima al-fihri, a muslim woman who used her inheritance to create something that has outlasted empires. we ended in chefchaouen — the famous blue city nestled in the rif mountains. dinner at the riad, breakfast arranged in the morning, and then the whole day was ours to explore freely. no guide, just a curated list of must-see spots and good vibes. marrakesh is beautiful. but morocco is so much more than one city. @getyourguide moroccotravel moroccoitinerary moroccotravelguide visitmorocco imperialcitiesmorocco​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

 7. **el badi palace**: although in ruins, this former palace offers insight into the opulence that once was. don't miss the stork nests on the walls!  8. **marrakech museum**: housed in a former palace, this museum features moroccan art, historical artifacts, and a tranquil courtyard.  9. **atlas mountains**: a short trip from marrakesh, the atlas mountains offer breathtaking scenery and outdoor activities like hiking and exploring berber villages.  10. **traditional hammam**: experience a traditional moroccan bath at a local hammam, a unique cultural experience that will leave you refreshed! 🇲🇦 15 things to do in marrakech ⬇️ 🇲🇦➡️ get my free marrakech travel guide at marrakech.kelseyinlondon.com (link in bio) which includes:📍places to visit ⭐️ top things to do & activities 🍽️ best restaurants 🏩 hotel & riad recommendations 📆 best time to visit 🚕 how to get around⁣⁣ +  a saveable google map with all locations pinned 🗺️ 🇲🇦 15 things to do in marrakech ⬇️ 1. ben youssef medersa – this 16th-century islamic college once housed 900 students and looks more like a palace thanks to its intricate carvings and tiled courtyard.  2. souk semmarine – the beating heart of the medina, packed with handwoven rugs, ceramics, brass lanterns and mountains of saffron.  3. stay in a riad – skip the big hotels and book a traditional riad for a more authentic experience.  4. bacha coffee – one of the most beautiful cafés in marrakech, serving 200+ coffees and unreal pastries.  5. go quad biking – speed through palm groves and open plains, stopping for tea before heading back out. 6. have a spa day – marrakech is famous for its hammams. book a steam bath and massage at your riad or opt for a spa package with transfers. 7. medina street food tour – try pastille, msemen pancakes, olives, sweets and tagine. arrive hungry – you’ll eat a lot! 8. jardin majorelle – these these iconic blue gardens were created by jacques majorelle and later owned by yves saint laurent. 9. agafay desert – spend a night glamping under the stars just an hour from marrakech.  10. marrakech museum – inside dar menebhi palace, showcasing traditional and modern moroccan art.  11. le jardin – have lunch at this dreamy restaurant – make sure to order the pastille.  12. jemaa el-fnaa – the city’s buzzing main square filled with stalls, performers and street food. 13. saadian tombs – hidden until 1917, home to sultan ahmad al-mansur and 60 royals, with marble, gold and zellige tiles. 14. dardar rooftop – sunset cocktails with dj sets and medina views.  15. atlas mountains – day trip to ourika’s waterfalls, berber villages, riverside tagine lunch and a women’s argan co-op. 🇲🇦 2 days in marrakech morocco | things to do in marrakech | marrakech restaurants | marrakech cafes | marrakech bars | hotels in marrakech | marrakech food spots | marrakech travel tips | marrakech hidden gems | marrakech activities | where to eat marrakech. places to visit in marrakech. weekend in marrakech.

5 day itinerary in january - fly with me to morocco, marrakech :) 🇲🇦 marrakech traveltok fyp  explore an exciting 5-day itinerary in marrakech this january. get tips on your morocco adventure and travel planning! marrakech traveltok  discovering the magic of marrakech: a 5-day itinerary 🇲🇦  if you're dreaming of a vibrant getaway, look no further than marrakech, morocco! in january, i embarked on a captivating 5-day adventure that turned out to be an unforgettable experience. here's a detailed breakdown of my journey, including all the highlights that made my trip special.    day 1: arrival and exploring the city i flew into marrakech, and the total cost for flights and a centrally located airbnb was just £220. what a steal for five fantastic days! 🌞 after settling in, my first exploration took me from the stunning koutoubia mosque to jemaa el fna, the main square buzzing with energy and life. be prepared to haggle! it’s all part of the experience—but don’t worry, the fresh juice stalls with refreshments under £2 make it worthwhile. one of the must-visit spots is the dardar rooftop restaurant, where the sunset views paired with delicious cuisine are simply breathtaking; we indulged in a wonderful meal for only £19 each.    day 2: gardens and adventure on my second day, i visited the enchanting jardin majorelle, a botanical paradise, which is conveniently next to the ysl museum. as a student, i snagged a combined ticket for just £15! 🌼 later, i was off to the agafay desert for some thrilling adventures. riding camels, quad biking at sunset, and enjoying a delectable buffet with fire shows—all for £30—made the day unforgettable!    day 3: thrills in the atlas mountains day three pushed my limits as i ventured to the atlas mountains. we zipped along a rope above stunning landscapes and ziplined from mountain to mountain, soaking in spectacular views. 🌄 this adrenaline-pumping experience cost £40 and led us to a lovely breakfast in a quaint valley. i also visited the bahia palace, known for its incredible architecture, and took a memorable horseback ride.    day 4: waterfalls and fine dining the beauty continued on day four when i took a £20 trip to mesmerizing waterfalls. i even got up close on a boat ride and met an adorable monkey! 🐒 to cap off the day, i indulged in a luxurious dinner at nouba. the ambiance was fantastic, and while it was a bit pricier, it was worth a visit for that special experience—though perhaps not a must-do unless you're after some luxury vibes.   day 5: a stunning sunrise waking up at 5:00 am was so worth it for the hot air balloon ride over the atlas mountains. 🌅 while waiting, i enjoyed the company of friendly dogs. the view from above was extraordinary, despite the playful choice of background music! the brunch afterwards was an absolute 10/10, and while this was the most expensive activity at £99, it was truly a highlight. after a quick visit to the saadian tombs for £8, it was nearly time to head back to the uk.   total cost and final thoughts in total, along with food and transport, my five-day marrakech adventure came to about £650 each—a reasonable investment for such rich experiences.   if you're looking for holiday ideas or travel inspiration, marrakech is a wonderful option! marrakech is best experienced in a riad  marrakech riad luxurytravels morocco  discover why staying in a marrakech riad offers authentic luxury and local charm—perfect for your morocco travel plans. marrakech riad luxurytravels morocco marrakech is best experienced in a riad. a riad places you inside the medina’s winding streets while offering a quiet, luxurious retreat centered around a courtyard or garden. choosing a riad means waking to birdsong, tiled fountains, and locally crafted décor—elements that reflect moroccan design and daily life.  why choose a riad in marrakech - central location: many riads sit within the old medina, putting souks, palaces, and historic sites within walking distance. this saves time and lets you explore narrow alleys that vehicles cannot reach. - authentic atmosphere: riads preserve traditional architecture—inner courtyards, mosaic tilework, carved wood, and rooftop terraces—creating an intimate, culturally rooted stay. - personal service: smaller than standard hotels, riads often provide attentive, personalized hospitality, from homemade breakfasts to tailored local recommendations.  what to expect in a riad - layout and amenities: expect inward-facing rooms around a central courtyard, often with a fountain or plants. many riads offer rooftop terraces with city views, private or shared lounges, and sometimes a small plunge pool. - food and dining: breakfast is commonly included and may feature moroccan staples like mint tea, fresh bread, and local pastries. some riads provide evening meals on request—ideal for tasting home-cooked moroccan cuisine. - atmosphere and privacy: riads are typically quiet sanctuaries despite medina bustle outside. rooms are designed for relaxation after days of sightseeing.  tips for booking and staying - location matters: verify the riad’s entrance is accessible and note proximity to major medina gates or landmarks to ease navigation. - read reviews: look for mentions of cleanliness, service, and accuracy of photos. pay attention to comments about noise or accessibility. - ask about amenities: confirm if the riad has heating or air conditioning, wi‑fi, rooftop access, or transport arrangements to and from the airport. - respect house rules: riads are often family-run; observe quiet hours and local customs.  enhancing your marrakech stay combine riad living with travel ideas like guided medina tours, visits to jardin majorelle, day trips to the atlas mountains, or food-focused strolls through souks. use rooftop moments for sunset photos or relaxed evenings sipping mint tea.  takeaway for an immersive, comfortable, and characterful stay in marrakech, a riad is the ideal choice. it blends authentic moroccan architecture, personalized service, and a peaceful base for exploring. planning ahead—location, reviews, and amenities—ensures the riad matches your travel style.  would you prefer a riad near the main souks or a quieter riad deeper in the medina? share your ideal riad feature and start planning your morocco trip.

hidden gems in marrakech - trust me, you won’t regret it;) 📍souk serajine (next to madrasa ben youssef) - for its craftsmanship and authenticity (especially if you’re looking for leather goods)  📍souk talaa (also in the madrasa ben youssef neighborhood) - the little place in the middle is beautiful and surrounded by so many cute shops, far away from the tourist traps 📍deserted photo booth - we discovered it late at night after dinner and had so much fun; the perfect little memory to take home  📍daraavalleytravel - if you’re looking for pottery, this is your spot ! everything is handmade by the owner himself (funny guy) and soooo pretty, almost bought the whole shop 📍route sidi abdelaziz - this is where you can find bacha coffee, and you have to go ! we went there on our way to the wa spa and discovered such a lovely neighborhood 📍amasmoud art - beautiful place if you’re looking for a rug, the owner is so sweet and has so many high quality pieces  discover marrakech's best kept secrets! explore authentic souks like souk serajine and souk talaa, find unique pottery at daraavalleytravel, and enjoy bacha coffee. your unforgettable marrakech adventure starts here! looking for the real marrakech beyond the usual tourist trails? this guide uncovers hidden gems that promise authenticity and unforgettable experiences. prepare to be delighted!  **souk serajine:** nestled next to the madrasa ben youssef, this souk is a treasure trove of craftsmanship. it's particularly noteworthy for its authentic leather goods, offering a genuine glimpse into traditional moroccan artistry.  **souk talaa:** also located in the vibrant madrasa ben youssef neighborhood, souk talaa offers a charming escape. the central area is exceptionally beautiful, surrounded by delightful shops that steer clear of typical tourist crowds.  **deserted photo booth:** a delightful discovery made late one night after dinner, this quirky photo booth provides a fun and unique way to capture memories from your trip. it's the perfect, lighthearted memento.  **daraavalleytravel:** for those seeking exquisite handmade pottery, this is the ultimate destination. the owner, a character in himself, crafts stunning pieces that are almost too beautiful to resist – you might find yourself wanting to buy the entire collection!  **route sidi abdelaziz:** this route leads to the iconic bacha coffee, a must-visit spot. while en route to the wa spa, a charming neighborhood unfolded, revealing this delightful destination.  **amasmoud art:** if you're in search of a beautiful rug, amasmoud art is your go-to. the owner's sweet demeanor complements the collection of high-quality rugs, showcasing remarkable artistry.  explore these special places and make your marrakech journey truly unique. you won't regret discovering these authentic corners of the city!

navigating the souks and enjoying the best experiences. morocco marrakechguide marrakech marrakech is a vibrant city full of rich cultural heritage, bustling markets, and stunning architecture. planning a 3-day itinerary can be overwhelming, but with a few essential tips, you can make the most of your visit to this enchanting destination. here are some helpful strategies to navigate your time in marrakech effectively.   pre-planning your visit one of the first tips for visiting marrakech is to pre-book your restaurants, especially if you're traveling in larger groups or only have a short trip. this ensures you won’t miss out on popular dining spots and can enjoy the local cuisine stress-free.   shopping in the souks when exploring the famous souks, make sure to practice your bartering skills. it's a customary practice in local markets, and you may find much better prices if you negotiate. also, wear comfortable footwear as you'll likely be walking around for an extended period.   respecting local customs while in marrakech, there are a few customs to be aware of. avoid taking photos of animals without permission, as some may charge you for it. it’s also advisable to dress conservatively during the day; consider wearing linen pants or long casual dresses to respect the local culture. evening attires at restaurants tend to be more relaxed, allowing for a bit more comfort.   connectivity and navigation purchasing a sim card at the airport can be beneficial for internet access when wifi isn’t available. use google maps to navigate the intricate paths of the souks, making sure you have a good sense of direction.   currency and payments always convert some money into dirhams upon arrival at the airport. while most restaurants accept cards, cash is generally preferred in the souks, especially for tips. there are also atms available should you need to withdraw more cash.   safe travel tips for an easier travel experience, particularly when using taxis, ensure that the meter is set before your journey or agree on a fee beforehand. hiring a private taxi for your stay can simplify your transportation needs.   hydration and safety do not drink tap water during your stay; opting for bottled water is a safer choice. lastly, be cautious about accepting directions or tours from strangers, and instead rely on trusted sources or your own navigation tools.   scenic dining experiences finally, don't miss the chance to visit a rooftop restaurant, especially during the day or at sunset, to take in the breathtaking views of the city. the combination of stunning landscapes and delicious cuisine can provide a memorable dining experience.  with these tips in hand, your 3-day adventure in marrakech will be both enjoyable and enriching. embrace the unique culture and hospitality that marrakech has to offer, and make the most out of your visit!

at the market, an age-old tradition reflecting the moroccan culture.  for lunch, enjoy a meal at **le slimana**, known for its delicious moroccan dishes, where you can savor fresh ingredients and rich flavors. after recharging, take a leisurely **walk through the market**, exploring the various stalls and soaking in the lively atmosphere.  no visit to marrakech is complete without exploring the stunning **madrasa ben youssef**, an ancient islamic school known for its intricate moroccan architecture. this site offers a glimpse into the educational practices of the past and the beauty of moroccan design.  to wrap up your day, consider taking a refreshing swim before dressing up for a memorable dinner and show at **comptoir darna**. here, you’ll enjoy a blend of exquisite moroccan cuisine and captivating entertainment with traditional music and dance.  this itinerary promises not just sightseeing but a day filled with unique experiences that reveal the charm of marrakech. for an unforgettable travel experience, don't forget to immerse yourself in the city’s vibrant culture and rich history.

one of the prettiest villages in the mountains of morocco chefchaouen morocco placestovisit moroccotravel bluepearl  discover chefchaouen, the blue pearl in morocco's mountains: top sights, best photo spots, and travel tips for visiting this picturesque village. chefchaouen morocco placestovisit moroccotravel bluepearl lead chefchaouen — often called the blue pearl — is one of the prettiest mountain villages in morocco. nestled in the rif mountains, its lanes, bluewashed buildings, and scenic viewpoints make it a top destination for photographers and travelers seeking a compact, colorful moroccan experience.  what makes chefchaouen special - distinctive blue-painted medina streets that create striking photo opportunities.  - mountain setting offering nearby hikes, panoramic viewpoints, and cooler air than coastal cities. - a compact, walkable old town with artisanal shops, local cafes, and traditional moroccan architecture.  top sights and photo spots - medina alleys: wander narrow lanes lined with blue walls, doors, and stairways at every turn. perfect for morning or late-afternoon light. - plaza uta el-hammam: the central square for people-watching, dining, and gateway views into the medina. - kasbah and gardens: a historic fortress with a small garden and museum; climb nearby streets for elevated views. - outskirts viewpoints: short hikes from the town reveal sweeping views of the blue roofs against mountain backdrops.  practical travel tips - best time to visit: spring and autumn for mild weather; summer can be busy but still popular. early morning avoids crowds and captures softer light for photos.  - getting there: chefchaouen is commonly reached by road from tangier, fez, or casablanca. buses, shared taxis, and private transfers are typical options.  - accommodation: a range of guesthouses and riads inside and around the medina — choose one near the center to maximize walking access.  - respect local customs: modest dress helps in conservative areas; ask before photographing people.  - shopping: look for woven blankets, pottery, leather goods, and local crafts in the medina. haggling is common but polite bargaining yields fair prices.  nearby activities - day hikes in the rif mountains for nature and panoramic views.  - short excursions to nearby waterfalls, rural villages, or coastal cities if you have extra time.  closing / call to action chefchaouen’s blue lanes and mountain setting offer a memorable, photogenic stop on any morocco itinerary. plan for a relaxed pace, carry a camera for the iconic blue scenes, and leave time to wander; the village’s charm reveals itself in its streets and views. have you visited chefchaouen or planning a trip? consider which photo spots you most want to see and when you’ll go to avoid the crowds.  chefchaouen morocco placestovisit moroccotravel bluepearl
