walked between two continents, chased blue waterfalls, and stood beside one of iceland’s most powerful waterfalls—all in a single day. 🇮🇸✨ 📍 golden circle: þingvellir • brúarfoss • gullfoss would you add iceland to your bucket list?   iceland visiticeland icelandroadtrip exploreiceland selfiebucketlist @icelandair  explore þingvellir, walk between tectonic plates, visit brúarfoss’s blue falls and gullfoss’s powerful cascade in one day on the golden circle. iceland visiticeland icelandroadtrip lead start your iceland golden circle day trip in reykjavík at hallgrímskirkja church and rainbow street, then drive the classic loop to þingvellir national park, brúarfoss and gullfoss—three unforgettable stops in a single day.  begin: reykjavík photo start park near hallgrímskirkja and frame the church at the end of the colourful rainbow street for the best photo. from reykjavík, head east on the ring road toward the golden circle.  þingvellir national park — stand between continents first major stop is þingvellir national park. walk down into almannagjá fissure to literally stand between the north american and eurasian tectonic plates. for an easier, flatter approach and closer access to oxarárfoss, drive to p3 parking rather than the main visitor centre. one parking payment covers both p1 and p3.  oxarárfoss waterfall and flossajökull canyon a short walk from parking leads to oxarárfoss, a waterfall framed by dramatic basalt rock formations. continue to the flossajökull canyon viewpoint—the same view featured on the park’s visitor brochure—for sweeping canyon scenery.  brúarfoss — the blue waterfall next drive to the brúarfoss parking area. a short walk from the lot brings you to brúarfoss, celebrated for its vivid blue water and striking cascades. the trail is rewarding and relatively short from the lot, making it an ideal stop on a day itinerary.  gullfoss — the golden falls finish the loop at gullfoss, one of iceland’s most powerful waterfalls. local legend says a wealthy farmer named sævar (recounted here as jagger) threw his gold into the falls to keep others from inheriting it—giving gullfoss its name, the golden falls. the scale and force of gullfoss provide a dramatic close to the day.  practical tips - start early from reykjavík to fit all stops comfortably in one day. - use p3 parking at þingvellir for easier access to oxarárfoss and a flatter trail. - one parking fee covers both p1 and p3. - bring a waterproof layer and sturdy shoes; paths can be wet and uneven. - allocate extra time at brúarfoss and gullfoss for photos and viewpoints.  close / call to action this single-day golden circle route packs tectonic rifts, basalt-framed waterfalls, vivid blue cascades and one of iceland’s most powerful falls into a memorable day. would you add iceland to your travel bucket list? identified languages: ["en"]

outer layers: windproof and waterproof jacket and pants are non-negotiable. icelandic weather is famously variable.  - mid layers: wool or fleece to retain warmth even when wet.  - base layers: fast-drying materials for comfort during active days.  - accessories: beanie, gloves, woolen socks (yes, even in july), and sturdy waterproof boots for hiking and uneven terrain.  - sun and swim: sunglasses, sunscreen and a bathing suit for hot springs, pools, and geothermal lagoons.  top activities in july - kayaking and snorkeling: explore clear waters and dramatic coastlines.  - glacier experiences: walk on glaciers or join guided glacier tours and ice cave visits where available.  - glacier lagoon tours: boat among icebergs and photograph luminous ice.  - midnight sun experiences: try a midnight sun horse ride, buggy tour, or late-evening hike.  - hot springs and geothermal pools: a perfect way to unwind after an active day.  practical tips - layers: dress in layers so you can add or remove warmth quickly as weather shifts.  - footwear: waterproof, supportive boots for trails and wet conditions.  - sleep: bring a sleeping mask to cope with near-constant daylight.  - gear care: choose fast-drying and quick-wicking fabrics to stay comfortable.  - safety: follow local guidance for glacier and coastal activities and book guided tours where required.  questions and planning if you’re planning a july trip, think about which activities matter most—birdwatching and coastal wildlife, glacier adventures, or long hikes—and pack accordingly. drop specific questions about routes, gear, or bookings to get tailored advice.  enjoying iceland in july means trading darkness for endless opportunities: long days, active wildlife, and a full range of outdoor adventures. pack smart, layer up, and make the most of the midnight sun.  icelandinjuly iceland visiticeland

visiting seljalandsfoss is unforgettable because you can walk behind the waterfall—a rare and misty experience. below is a practical itinerary to help you plan, including how to fit in nearby highlights. 🗺️ quick itinerary options half-day (from reykjavík): · drive ~1.5 hours on route 1 → seljalandsfoss (1 hour) → gljúfrabúi (hidden waterfall, 30 min) → return. full-day (south coast classics): · seljalandsfoss → skógafoss (30 min east) → reynisfjara black sand beach → return to reykjavík. ✨ top experiences · walk behind the falls (apr–oct recommended; path can be icy in winter). you will get wet—wear a waterproof jacket and boots. protect your camera. · visit gljúfrabúi – a hidden cascade inside a canyon, a 5-minute walk away. wading through a shallow stream or using stepping stones leads to the best view. · sunset photography – the golden light facing east makes for stunning shots. ⏰ timing & seasons · summer (may–sep): light until late; paths fully open. · winter (nov–feb): frozen surroundings, fewer crowds, but access behind the falls may be closed. crampons recommended. 🚗 practical info · parking: 1,000 isk (~$7 usd), payable by app or machine. toilets and a small snack kiosk available. · distance from reykjavík: ~120 km (75 miles) – easy day trip. 📸 pro tips · arrive before 9 am or after 7 pm to avoid bus tour crowds. · don’t skip the sunset – the evening glow is magical. · combine with nearby falls: add gljúfrabúi (5 min walk) and skógafoss (30 min drive). creatorsearchinsights iceland waterfall seljalandsfoss nature  plan your unforgettable visit to seljalandsfoss with this practical itinerary and top experiences. discover nearby highlights for a perfect trip! iceland waterfall seljalandsfoss visiting seljalandsfoss in iceland promises an unforgettable experience as you can walk behind one of the country’s most iconic waterfalls. this rare opportunity allows you to feel the cool mist on your face and take in breathtaking views of the cascading water from a unique perspective.   quick itinerary options for those planning a trip to seljalandsfoss, here are some suggested itineraries: - **half-day (from reykjavík):**    - drive approximately 1.5 hours on route 1 to reach seljalandsfoss (1 hour visit) and then head to gljúfrabúi, a hidden waterfall just 30 minutes away before returning to reykjavík. - **full-day (south coast classics):**    - after seljalandsfoss, continue your adventure to skógafoss (30 minutes east) and then to reynisfjara black sand beach before returning to reykjavík.   top experiences - **walk behind the falls:** this experience is highly recommended from april to october. keep in mind that paths can be icy during winter, and you'll likely get wet, so wearing a waterproof jacket and boots is essential. don't forget to protect your camera! - **visit gljúfrabúi:** this hidden gem is located just a 5-minute walk away from seljalandsfoss. to reach the best viewing point, you may need to wade through a shallow stream or use stepping stones. - **sunset photography:** the golden light facing east creates perfect conditions for stunning photographs, particularly during sunset.   timing & seasons - **summer (may–september):** enjoy long daylight hours, with all paths open for exploration. - **winter (november–february):** experience a unique beauty with fewer crowds and frozen surroundings, but be aware that access behind the falls might be restricted. crampons are highly recommended during this season.   practical information - **parking:** fees are around 1,000 isk (~$7 usd), which can be paid via an app or at a machine. essential facilities, including toilets and a small snack kiosk, are available on-site. - **distance from reykjavík:** seljalandsfoss is roughly 120 km (75 miles) from the capital, making it an easy day trip.   pro tips - to avoid crowds caused by bus tours, aim to arrive before 9 am or after 7 pm. - be sure to witness the sunset, known for its magical evening glow. - don’t miss the nearby falls: gljúfrabúi is just a 5-minute walk from seljalandsfoss, and skógafoss is only a 30-minute drive away.  exploring seljalandsfoss and the surrounding natural wonders offers an enriching experience for nature lovers and adventure seekers alike. prepare for an unforgettable journey in iceland!
